title: 頭先新聞講話強制舉報虐兒

replies.msg:
有冇人覺得其實咁樣做係將啲責任推畀前線嘅工作人員.....

如果真係行呢一套嘅話其實影響嘅唔單只係社褔界仲有其他界別人仕...

雖然呢樣嘢係外國行咗好耐, 但係人哋外國可能一個人睇幾個cases, 但係你嚮香港可能一個人睇幾十個cases

宜家感覺好似係出咗事, 政府懶係做啲嘢, 跟住以後如果再出事嘅時候就可以將啲責任推撚曬畀前線, 其實都幾撚仆街.

其實就算行左強制通報之後, 就算真係再有虐兒cases , d高層係咪真係冇責任呢?
其實會唔會係資源不足或者唔夠人做嘢?

我覺得如果真係出咗事, 要追求嘅唔單只係前線員工仲有佢嘅上司都要追究, 咁樣先至fair.

同埋你行咗呢套之後, 其實要影嚮嘅唔單只社褔界......例如啲醫護人員都有影響, 有機會上至醫生, 下至掃地嗰個見到個小朋友可能有瘀傷或者有其他sign 你冇去通報嘅話, 最後可以法律追究返你.

但係可能事實係你要一個睇幾廿條友, 或者係你得一分鐘去 assess每一個patient , 但係去到出咗事啦, 法律追究都唔會去到啲高層
不嬲都係上職領功，下層食屎

replies.msg: 屌你老母咪撚用殘體字啦

其實外國係一樣有類似case係有好多社會關注嘅曾經....


In 2000 in London, an eight-year-old Ivorian girl, Victoria Adjo Climbié (2 November 1991 – 25 February 2000), was tortured and murdered by her guardians. Her death led to a public inquiry and produced major changes in child protection policies in the United Kingdom............

After Climbié's death, the parties involved in her case were widely criticised. A public inquiry, headed by Lord Laming, was ordered. It discovered numerous instances where Climbié could have been saved, noted that many of the organisations involved in her care were badly run, and discussed the racial aspects surrounding the case........


The Laming report, published on 28 January 2003, found that the agencies involved in her care had failed to protect her, and that on at least twelve occasions, workers involved in her case could have prevented her death, particularly condemning the senior managers involved


condemning the senior managers involved <= 人哋外國係會追究埋d高層

Previously each local authority managed their own child protection register, a list of children believed to be at risk, and no national register existed; this, combined with local authorities' tendency to suppress information about child abuse cases, led to the implementation of the child database <= 你香港聲稱話會改善, 會唔會做呢啲硬件嘢先 ???

The 1999 Department of Health document, Working Together to Safeguard Children (now superseded), set out child protection guidance to doctors, nurses, and midwives. The Royal College of Nursing, however, said that there was evidence that many nurses did not receive proper training in these areas <= 香港嗰班撚樣高層會唔會真係provide 足夠 training畀你?

Denise Platt, chief inspector of the social services inspectorate (SSI), said doctors, police officers and teachers often thought their only responsibility was to help social services, forgetting that they had a distinct role to play <= 你一立法咩醫生呀, 護士呀, 連警犬都會involve


After the inquiry, there was a feeling that senior managers had managed to escape responsibility and that only junior staff members were punished <= 連英國都係咁, 你可想而知香港會幾仆街
